requirements.summary: Add React Query mutation hook for marking payment success
acceptanceCriteria: useMarkPaymentSuccess mutation calls the backend markPaymentSuccess function.
On success, the customers query cache is invalidated so all consumers re-fetch fresh data.
The hook is exported from useQueries.ts for use in AdminPage and ReceiptPage.

file_operations.description: Add a new mutation hook `useMarkPaymentSuccess` that calls the backend `markPaymentSuccess(customerId: bigint)` function and invalidates the 'customers' query key on success to trigger automatic refresh of customer data across all components.

requirements.summary: Add 'Mark as Paid' button to AdminPage with payment status handling
acceptanceCriteria: Each customer row in AdminPage shows a 'Mark as Paid' button when paymentStatus is not 'success'.
Button enters loading state during mutation and is replaced with a green 'Paid' badge after success.
The receiptId value (e.g., VOC17092026) is visible in the admin row after being set.
Already-paid customers show the Paid badge and receiptId without a 'Mark as Paid' button.

file_operations.description: Add a 'Mark as Paid' button for each customer whose paymentStatus is not 'success'. Use the `useMarkPaymentSuccess` mutation from useQueries.ts. Show loading state during the mutation and display a green 'Paid ✅' badge after success. Display the receiptId field alongside customer details once it is set. For customers already marked as paid, show only the badge and receiptId without the button.

requirements.summary: Create ReceiptPage with payment receipt display and auto-refresh
acceptanceCriteria: ReceiptPage renders all required fields: customer name, mobile, service name, amount, UPI ID 8173064549@okicici, receiver name, transaction date/time, and receiptId.
Header displays 'Vijay Online Centre' and 'Successful Payment Receipt'.
Payment status shows a green 'SUCCESSFUL ✅' badge.
When paymentStatus is 'pending', the page shows a pending state and polls the backend every 5 seconds.
Once paymentStatus becomes 'success', the receipt renders automatically without a manual page refresh.

file_operations.description: Create a new page that accepts a customerId or mobile number from URL params/query. Fetch the customer record using useQuery. When paymentStatus is 'success', render a clean receipt displaying: header ('Vijay Online Centre' and 'Successful Payment Receipt'), customer name, mobile, service name, amount with ₹ symbol, payment method (UPI QR), UPI ID (8173064549@okicici, hardcoded), receiver name (Vijay Online Centre, hardcoded), transaction date/time from paymentDate, and receiptId. When paymentStatus is 'pending', show a waiting message and use setInterval to refetch the customer data every 5 seconds until payment is confirmed. Use React Query's refetch capability and cleanup the interval on unmount or when status changes to 'success'.

requirements.summary: Style ReceiptPage with clean print-optimized design
acceptanceCriteria: Receipt is displayed on a white background with center-aligned header.
A large green 'SUCCESS' badge/text is prominently shown.
Customer details and payment details are in clearly separated, labeled sections.
Receipt number is displayed at the bottom.
@media print CSS hides header navigation, buttons, and footer so only the receipt content prints.

file_operations.description: Apply inline styles or Tailwind classes for a clean white background, center-aligned header with bold business name, and a large green 'SUCCESS ✅' text at the top. Organize customer and payment details into clearly separated sections using card-like divs with labels. Display the receiptId prominently at the bottom. Use the existing navy/saffron color palette for branding accents. Add @media print CSS rules (scoped style tag or global index.css) to hide navigation, buttons, and footer elements during print, ensuring only the receipt content is printed.

requirements.summary: Add Print and Download PDF buttons to ReceiptPage
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Print Receipt' button calls window.print() and only the receipt content is printed.
A 'Download Slip' button generates and downloads a PDF file of the receipt.
The downloaded file is named using the pattern VOC-Receipt-{receiptId}.pdf.
Both buttons are hidden during print (@media print).

file_operations.description: Add a 'Print Receipt' button that calls window.print(). Add a 'Download Slip' button that uses html2canvas and jsPDF (or browser print-to-PDF flow) to generate a PDF of the receipt content and trigger download with filename 'VOC-Receipt-{receiptId}.pdf'. Hide both buttons using @media print CSS rules. If using html2canvas/jsPDF, install dependencies via npm and import them; if using browser print-to-PDF, guide the user to use Print > Save as PDF.

requirements.summary: Add mobile number search feature to ReceiptPage
acceptanceCriteria: A mobile number input field is visible on the ReceiptPage when no customerId is pre-filled.
Entering a valid mobile number and submitting fetches and displays the matching receipt.
If no matching paid record is found, a clear 'No paid receipt found for this mobile number' message is shown.
If the record exists but paymentStatus is pending, a 'Payment pending' message is shown.

file_operations.description: Add a mobile number search input at the top of the page. When no customerId is present in the URL, show the search input. On form submission, find the customer by mobile number from the getAllCustomers query result. If a matching customer with paymentStatus='success' is found, display the receipt. If the customer is found but paymentStatus is 'pending', show a 'Payment pending' message. If no matching record is found, show 'No paid receipt found for this mobile number'. Allow searching again after displaying a result.

Add a query hook `useGetAllCustomers` that fetches all customers via `getAllCustomers()` backend method. This allows ReceiptPage to search for a customer by mobile number when no customerId is provided in the URL.

requirements.summary: Redirect to ReceiptPage after payment success
acceptanceCriteria: After the payment step in PaymentPage shows success, the user is automatically navigated to the ReceiptPage.
The customerId is passed as a parameter so the receipt can be loaded directly.
The ReceiptPage correctly loads the customer record using the passed customerId.

file_operations.description: After the customer submission succeeds and the success step is displayed, add an automatic redirect to the ReceiptPage using React Router's navigate function. Pass the newly created customerId as a URL parameter (e.g., /receipt?customerId=123 or /receipt/123 depending on the route structure). Optionally add a short delay (1-2 seconds) so the user sees the success message before redirecting.

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to '/receipt' renders ReceiptPage without requiring login.
Query parameters (customerId or mobile) are read and used to load the appropriate receipt.
The route is added to App.tsx alongside existing home/payment/admin routes.

file_operations.description: Add a new route for ReceiptPage at path '/receipt' (or '/receipt/:customerId' if using path params). Ensure the route is accessible without admin authentication. Place the route alongside existing HomePage, PaymentPage, and AdminPage routes. The route should accept optional query parameters for customerId and mobile number that ReceiptPage will read and use to load the appropriate receipt.
